summary: Patrick briefly explains that he misconfigured his DNS records when migrating his blog, accidentally creating a CNAME record pointing to Google instead of an MX record. This caused the non-www version of kalzumeus.com to go down. He notes it will take about a week for the TTL to expire and the fix to propagate. He also set up a redirect via Google Webmaster Console as a stopgap.
key_points: A DNS misconfiguration (CNAME vs MX record) took down the non-www version of the blog after migration
DNS TTL propagation means the fix will take up to a week to fully resolve across the internet
Google Webmaster Console redirect was set up as an interim mitigation
key_insight: DNS misconfigurations during blog migrations are easy to make and slow to fix due to TTL propagation delays.
